sourceNote: MVP educational summary based on public election-process material from ECI, NVSP, and data.gov.in.

steps.title: Announcement
steps.description: The election authority announces the election schedule and key dates.
steps.detail: This stage sets the formal calendar for nominations, campaigning, polling, counting, and result declaration.

steps.title: Nomination
steps.description: Candidates file nomination papers within the official window.
steps.detail: Nomination documents are reviewed so only eligible candidates move forward to the contest.

steps.title: Campaign
steps.description: Candidates and parties share their policies with voters under campaign rules.
steps.detail: Campaigning helps voters compare options while model conduct rules guide fair behavior.

steps.title: Voting
steps.description: Voters cast their ballots at assigned polling stations.
steps.detail: Polling stations manage identity checks, queues, ballot secrecy, accessibility, and orderly voting.

steps.title: Counting
steps.description: Votes are counted and the winning candidate is declared.
steps.detail: Counting is conducted under official supervision before final results are published.
